overview: This dragonfly appears to be a species within the skimmer family (Libellulidae), which includes types like saddlebag gliders or pennants. The insect is captured mid-air, likely exhibiting ovipositing behavior, where it releases eggs by dipping its abdomen into the water. It has distinctive dark patches at the base or tips of its wings, a common feature for identifying species in this family. iNaturalist +4 5 sites Metallic Pennant (Idiataphe cubensis) · iNaturalist Metallic Pennant (Idiataphe cubensis) · iNaturalist. The insect is captured mid-air, likely exhibiting ovipositing behavior, where it releases eggs by dipping its abdomen into the water.
It has distinctive dark patches at the base or tips of its wings, a common feature for identifying species in this family.
